steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Apply the surface area formula for a rectangular prism
steps.content:
A rectangular prism has six faces in three distinct pairs. We sum the area of each pair and multiply by 2:

Calculate each distinct face area with $l = 26$ cm, $w = 17$ cm, and $h = 14$ cm:
$$lw = 26 \times 17 = 442$$
$$lh = 26 \times 14 = 364$$
$$wh = 17 \times 14 = 238$$

steps.title: Sum the face areas and multiply by 2
